claim: EVA 'q' maps to Syriac waw, producing the conjunction wa- ('and') at 14.9% of tokens — a frequency consistent with attested Syriac prose.
evidence_for: 14.9% matches published Syriac prose wa- frequency
q is known to be heavily position-constrained in Voynichese (initial only)
evidence_against: q initial frequency could match many high-frequency features; single-datapoint match
No independent test beyond self-consistency with hypothesis
test: Measure: what fraction of tokens begin with EVA 'q'? Compare to 14.9% prediction
tests_remaining: Measure: what fraction of tokens begin with EVA 'q'? Compare to 14.9% prediction
source: Brady Defibaugh (April 2026), A Syriac Pharmaceutical Dispensatory Encoded in the Voynich Manuscript (DANI pipeline v31)
